assignment.objective:
Do a comprehensive survey of all MVP/prototype hacks in the Rally project that need to be removed for production. Search for and report:

1. `backend/src/middleware/auth.ts` — the NODE_ENV/development bypass on line 20
2. `frontend/Rally/src/store/slices/authSlice.ts` — isAuthenticated: true hack
3. All files with hardcoded `http://localhost:3001` URLs — search backend/src/ and frontend/Rally/src/
4. All files with `@ts-nocheck` — search frontend/Rally/src/
5. `frontend/Rally/App.tsx` — the notification init that references old registerPushToken signature
6. `backend/.env` — check for NODE_ENV and MVP_MODE settings
7. `frontend/Rally/src/services/` — any services with hardcoded localhost URLs instead of env vars
8. Any remaining `TODO`, `FIXME`, `mock`, `fake` references in backend/src/ and frontend/Rally/src/
9. `backend/src/server.ts` — check if CSP/helmet config is too permissive for production

Report a structured list: file, line, issue, and recommended fix for each item found.

agents.result:
Good, I now have all the data needed. Let me compile the comprehensive report.

---

### SUMMARY
I performed a comprehensive survey of all 9 categories of MVP/prototype hacks in the Rally project. Every item requested was found and verified with file:line evidence. The project has widespread hardcoded localhost URLs (21 hits in frontend, 11 in backend), 33 files with `@ts-nocheck`, an auth bypass in middleware, a hardcoded `isAuthenticated: true` in Redux state, a notification signature mismatch, permissive CSP config, mock/fake data in production screens, and no `.env` `MVP_MODE` variable despite code checking for it.

### EVIDENCE

**1. Auth middleware NODE_ENV/MVP bypass**
- `backend/src/middleware/auth.ts:20` — `if (process.env.MVP_MODE === 'true' || process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development')` skips all JWT auth, assigns guest user.
- `backend/.env:2` — `NODE_ENV=development` is set; no `MVP_MODE` key exists at all, so the `MVP_MODE === 'true'` check is dead code.

**2. Redux isAuthenticated: true hack**
- `frontend/Rally/src/store/slices/authSlice.ts:41` — `isAuthenticated: true, // MVP mode` in initial state. This means the app always believes the user is logged in, even without credentials.
- `frontend/Rally/src/navigation/AppNavigator.tsx:55-67` — `RootNavigator` reads `isAuthenticated` from Redux and renders `Main` (authenticated) stack whenever true; the `registerPushToken` call at line 62 passes `(userId || '', deviceId || '')` instead of the single-argument signature `registerPushToken(deviceId: string)`.

**3. Hardcoded `http://localhost:3001` URLs**

| File | Line | Code |
|---|---|---|
| `backend/src/utils/fileUpload.ts` | 71 | `const baseUrl = process.env.API_BASE_URL \|\| 'http://localhost:3001'` |
| `backend/src/routes/shareCard.ts` | 44 | `const appUrl = process.env.FRONTEND_URL \|\| 'http://localhost:3001'` |
| `backend/src/routes/mvpSessions.ts` | 426 | ``shareLink: `${process.env.FRONTEND_URL \|\| 'http://localhost:3001'}/join/...` `` |
| `backend/src/routes/mvpSessions.ts` | 1000 | ``shareUrl: `${process.env.FRONTEND_URL \|\| 'http://localhost:3001'}/session/...` `` |
| `backend/src/services/oauthService.ts` | 26 | ``redirectUri: ... \|\| 'http://localhost:3001'/api/v1/oauth/google/callback`` |
| `backend/src/services/oauthService.ts` | 35 | Same pattern for WeChat callback |
| `backend/src/routes/webSession.ts` | 578 | `window.location.replace('http://localhost:3001' + ...)` — bare string, no env fallback |
| `frontend/Rally/src/config/api.ts` | 6 | `(__DEV__ ? 'http://localhost:3001/api/v1' : '/api/v1')` — `__DEV__` flag leak |
| `frontend/Rally/src/config.ts` | 10,13 | Returns `'http://localhost:3001'` via `Platform.OS` switching, no env-var escape |
| `frontend/Rally/src/services/apiService.ts` | 21 | `constructor(baseURL: string = 'http://localhost:3001/api/v1')` |
| `frontend/Rally/src/services/apiClient.ts` | 2 | `const API_BASE = process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_API_URL \|\| 'http://localhost:3001/api/v1'` |
| `frontend/Rally/src/services/analyticsApi.ts` | 13 | `const API_BASE_URL = 'http://localhost:3001/api/v1'` — no env fallback |
| `frontend/Rally/src/services/NotificationService.ts` | 222, 251, 276 | Three separate `const API_BASE_URL = 'http://localhost:3001/api/v1'` inline in methods |
| `frontend/Rally/src/services/mvpApiService.ts` | 65 | `super(process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_API_URL \|\| 'http://localhost:3001/api/v1')` |
| `frontend/Rally/src/hooks/useMessaging.ts` | 6 | `const SOCKET_URL = 'http://localhost:3001'` — bare string, no env |
| `frontend/Rally/src/screens/PlayerProfileScreen.tsx` | 19 | `const API_BASE_URL = 'http://localhost:3001/api/v1'` |
| `frontend/Rally/src/screens/LiveGameScreen.tsx` | 31 | Same |
| `frontend/Rally/src/screens/JoinSessionScreen.tsx` | 17 | Same |
| `frontend/Rally/src/screens/CreateSessionScreen.tsx` | 78 | ``fetch(`http://localhost:3001/api/v1/session-templates/${deviceId}`)`` |
| `frontend/Rally/src/screens/SessionSettingsScreen.tsx` | 20 | Same pattern |
| `frontend/Rally/src/screens/SessionDetailScreen.tsx` | 27 | Same |
| `frontend/Rally/src/screens/LeaderboardScreen.tsx` | 14 | Same |
| `frontend/Rally/src/components/SessionSuggestions.tsx` | 43 | Same pattern |
| `frontend/Rally/src/components/ShareLinkHandler.tsx` | 56 | `const webFallbackUrl = 'http://localhost:3001/session/${shareCode}'` |
| `frontend/Rally/src/components/OrganizerClaimModal.tsx` | 24 | `const API_BASE_URL = 'http://localhost:3001/api/v1'` |
| `backend/src/__tests__/validation.js` | 72 | Test file (lower priority) |
| `backend/src/__tests__/mvpSessions.basic.test.ts` | 99, 206 | Test files (lower priority) |
| `backend/src/__tests__/mvpSessions.unit.test.ts` | 181 | Test file (lower priority) |

**4. `@ts-nocheck` files (33 total)**
- `frontend/Rally/src/navigation/AppNavigator.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/store/index.ts:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/screens/FriendsListScreen.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/screens/pairing/PairingScreen.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/screens/PlayerProfileScreen.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/screens/LiveGameScreen.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/screens/SessionHistoryScreen.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/screens/CreateSessionScreen.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/screens/SessionSettingsScreen.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/screens/SessionDetailScreen.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/screens/MySessionsScreen.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/screens/SessionOverviewScreen.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/screens/rotation/RotationScreen.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/screens/UserProfileScreen.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/screens/TrendsDashboardScreen.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/screens/SessionDiscoveryScreen.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/components/PredictionDashboardScreen.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/components/TournamentAnalyticsScreen.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/components/SessionSuggestions.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/components/SessionFilters.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/components/design-system/Layout/SessionHeader.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/components/design-system/Layout/PlayerCountIndicator.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/hooks/useNotificationManager.ts:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/services/NotificationService.ts:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/services/mvpApiService.ts:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/services/deviceService.ts:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/services/realTimeService.ts:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/services/apiService.ts:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/services/notificationApi.ts:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/theme/theme.ts:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/__tests__/QuickScoreRecordingScreen.test.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/hooks/__tests__/usePermissions.test.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/services/__tests__/statisticsApi.test.ts:1`

**5. Notification init with old signature**
- `frontend/Rally/App.tsx:17` — `await notificationService.registerPushToken(deviceId)` — single argument.
- `frontend/Rally/src/navigation/AppNavigator.tsx:62` — `await notificationService.registerPushToken(userId || '', deviceId || '')` — **two** arguments, but the actual method signature at `NotificationService.ts:215` is `async registerPushToken(deviceId: string)` (one argument). The AppNavigator call is a **signature mismatch**.
- `frontend/Rally/src/hooks/useNotificationManager.ts:57,67` — Defines its own local 2-arg `registerPushToken(pushToken, deviceId)` but calls `fetch` directly (not `notificationService.registerPushToken`) — a **different implementation** of the same concept. Confusingly, the `/notifications/register` endpoint it hits at `frontend/Rally/src/services/notificationApi.ts:56` expects `RegisterPushTokenRequest: { token, platform, deviceId }`.

**6. Backend .env**
- `backend/.env:2` — `NODE_ENV=development`
- `backend/.env` — **no `MVP_MODE` key exists**. The auth middleware checks `process.env.MVP_MODE === 'true'` but the variable is not defined in `.env`. It relies entirely on `NODE_ENV === 'development'`.

**7. Frontend services with hardcoded localhost URLs (non-env-var)**
- `frontend/Rally/src/services/analyticsApi.ts:13` — bare `const API_BASE_URL = 'http://localhost:3001/api/v1'` with no env fallback
- `frontend/Rally/src/services/NotificationService.ts:222,251,276` — three inline hardcoded `const API_BASE_URL = 'http://localhost:3001/api/v1'` inside methods
- `frontend/Rally/src/services/apiService.ts:21` — default parameter `'http://localhost:3001/api/v1'` (but `apiClient.ts:2` uses `process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_API_URL` fallback)
- `frontend/Rally/src/services/mvpApiService.ts:65` — has env fallback, so borderline acceptable
- `frontend/Rally/src/services/apiClient.ts:2` — has env fallback, borderline
- `frontend/Rally/src/services/notificationApi.ts:3` — imports `API_BASE_URL` from `../config/api`, which is the `__DEV__`-gated one

**8. TODO/FIXME/mock/fake references**
- **No `TODO` or `FIXME`** found in either backend/src or frontend/Rally/src (excluding test files).
- **`mock` in production code (non-test files):**
  - `frontend/Rally/src/screens/AchievementScreen.tsx:127` — `// Mock player ID - in real app, get from auth context` + `const playerId = 'player-123'`
  - `frontend/Rally/src/screens/PlayerProfileScreen.tsx:127,131,175,185` — `createMockProfile()` function used as fallback when API fails
  - `frontend/Rally/src/screens/SessionHistoryScreen.tsx:91,95,143,144` — `// Mock current user`, `const currentUserName = 'Alice Chen'`, `// Mock calculation`
  - `frontend/Rally/src/hooks/useLocation.ts:89,95` — `mockHasPermission`, `mockLocation` hardcoded coordinates (53.5444, -113.4909)
- **`fake` — no matches** in either src tree.

**9. CSP/helmet config in server.ts**
- `backend/src/server.ts:31-42` — helmet CSP directives:
  - `scriptSrc: ["'self'", "'unsafe-inline'", "'unsafe-eval'"]` — allows inline scripts and `eval()`, which is permissive and defeats XSS protection. `scriptSrcAttr: ["'unsafe-inline'"]` also allows inline event handlers.
  - No `frame-ancestors` directive (clickjacking risk)
  - No `upgrade-insecure-requests` directive
  - No `report-uri` or `report-to` for CSP violation monitoring
  - CORS at lines 47-57 allows any `localhost:*` origin via regex `/^http:\/\/localhost:\d+$/` — wide open in dev but dangerous if `NODE_ENV` accidentally left as development in production.

### CHANGES
None.

### RISKS

- **Auth bypass in production**: If `NODE_ENV` is not set to `production` (currently `development` in `.env`), every request gets a guest user. Mitigation: remove the entire `MVP_MODE`/`NODE_ENV` bypass block and set `NODE_ENV=production` in deployment.
- **CSP too permissive**: `'unsafe-eval'` and `'unsafe-inline'` script-src open the door to XSS. Mitigation: remove `'unsafe-eval'`, use nonces/hashes for inline scripts, add `frame-ancestors: 'none'`, and enforce HTTPS upgrade.
- **`@ts-nocheck` in 33 files**: Production code with type-checking disabled hides real bugs. Mitigation: remove each pragma and fix the underlying TypeScript errors.
- **`__DEV__` gate in `config/api.ts`**: This is a Metro/Expo global; if the app is built in release mode it falls through to `/api/v1` (relative), which is fine, but the `__DEV__` branch hardcodes localhost and is used by `notificationApi.ts`. Mitigation: remove the `__DEV__` ternary entirely, rely solely on `EXPO_PUBLIC_API_URL`.
- **Signature mismatch AppNavigator vs NotificationService**: `AppNavigator.tsx:62` calls `registerPushToken(userId, deviceId)` (2 args) but the method on `NotificationService.ts:215` takes only `(deviceId: string)`. This will silently pass `userId` as the deviceId and ignore the actual deviceId. Mitigation: unify to a single correct signature (the `notificationApi.ts` version expects `{ token, platform, deviceId }`).
- **Hardcoded `player-123` and `Alice Chen` in production screens**: AchievementScreen and SessionHistoryScreen use mock data as fallback logic. This produces incorrect UX in production. Mitigation: wire up real auth context and remove mock fallbacks.

### BLOCKERS
None.

assignment.objective:
Survey the messaging/chat infrastructure in the Rally project. Read these files and report exactly what exists:

1. `backend/prisma/schema.prisma` — search for Message, Conversation, Chat models. Report all fields.
2. `backend/src/routes/messaging.ts` — list all endpoints and their auth requirements
3. `backend/src/services/messagingService.ts` — list all methods
4. `backend/src/config/socket.ts` — search for messaging/chat-related socket events (sendMessage, typing, joinRoom, etc.)

6. `frontend/Rally/src/screens/ChatScreen.tsx` — how complete is the chat UI?

agents.result:
I now have all the data needed. Here's the complete report.

---

### SUMMARY
The Rally project has a well-scaffolded 1:1 / small-group messaging system with a full REST API, Socket.io real-time events, and polished React Native UI components. Both backend and frontend cover thread creation, message send/receive, typing indicators, read receipts, message deletion, search, and unread counts. However, the infrastructure is designed around direct message threads identified by participant lists — it lacks group chat concepts (named rooms, admin roles, pinned messages, media attachments, @mentions, read-by receipts per participant, group avatars, or push-to-talk). The socket server handles messaging events but the central `socketService.ts` on the frontend does NOT listen for or emit any chat events — the real-time chat logic lives entirely in a separate `useMessaging` hook with its own socket instance.

---

### EVIDENCE

- **`backend/prisma/schema.prisma:1386-1430`** — Two models:
  - `MessageThread`: `id (String @id)`, `participants (String[])`, `title (String?)`, `lastMessageAt (DateTime)`, `isActive (Boolean)`, and relation `messages Message[]`. Maps to `message_threads`. Indexes on `participants`, `lastMessageAt`, `isActive`.
  - `Message`: `id (String @id)`, `threadId (String)`, `senderId (String)`, `content (String)`, `messageType (MessageType, default TEXT)`, `sentAt (DateTime)`, `isRead (Boolean, default false)`, `readAt (DateTime?)`. Relations to `MessageThread` (Cascade) and `MvpPlayer` (sender). Maps to `messages`. Indexes on `threadId`, `senderId`, `sentAt`, `isRead`.
  - No `Conversation`, `GroupChat`, `GroupMember`, `GroupAdmin`, `GroupMessage` models.

- **`backend/src/routes/messaging.ts:1-290`** — 6 REST endpoints, ALL require `authenticateToken`:
  - `POST /threads` — create thread (participants + optional title)
  - `GET /threads` — list user's threads
  - `POST /messages` — send message (threadId, content, messageType)
  - `GET /threads/:threadId/messages` — paginate messages (limit/offset)
  - `POST /threads/:threadId/read` — mark messages as read
  - `GET /unread` — total unread count
  - `GET /threads/:threadId/unread` — per-thread unread count
  - `POST /threads/:threadId/leave` — leave a thread
  - `POST /threads/:threadId/participants` — add participants
  - `DELETE /messages/:messageId` — delete a message
  - `GET /threads/:threadId` — thread details
  - `GET /threads/:threadId/search` — search messages (query + limit)

- **`backend/src/services/messagingService.ts:1-290`** — 11 methods on `MessagingService`:
  - `createThread(data)` — deduplicates by exact participant set
  - `sendMessage(data)` — verifies participant membership, inserts, returns with sender name
  - `getThreadMessages(threadId, userId, limit, offset)` — paginated, chrono order
  - `getUserThreads(userId)` — with last message preview
  - `markMessagesAsRead(threadId, userId)` — bulk update
  - `getUnreadCount(userId)` — aggregate count
  - `getThreadUnreadCount(threadId, userId)` — per-thread count
  - `deleteMessage(messageId, userId)` — physical delete (owner-only)
  - `leaveThread(threadId, userId)` — removes from participants, deletes thread if empty
  - `addParticipants(threadId, userId, newParticipants)` — merges participant arrays
  - `getThreadDetails(threadId, userId)` — with message count
  - `searchMessages(threadId, userId, query, limit)` — ILIKE search
  - All use raw SQL (`$queryRaw`), not Prisma ORM.

- **`backend/src/config/socket.ts:259-375`** — Messaging socket events (server-side):
  - `messaging:join-thread` — joins Socket.io room `thread-{id}`
  - `messaging:leave-thread` — leaves room
  - `messaging:send-message` — saves to DB via `messagingService`, broadcasts `messaging:new-message` to room
  - `messaging:typing-start` — broadcasts `messaging:user-typing` to room (excludes sender)
  - `messaging:typing-stop` — broadcasts `messaging:user-stopped-typing` to room
  - `messaging:mark-read` — calls service, broadcasts `messaging:messages-read` to room
  - `messaging:delete-message` — calls service, broadcasts `messaging:message-deleted` to room
  - Error handling: `messaging:error` emitted back to sender on failures

- **`frontend/Rally/src/screens/ConversationListScreen.tsx:1-256`** — Fully built conversation list:
  - Pull-to-refresh, search/filter, FlatList with `ConversationCard`
  - New chat button → navigates to FriendsList to pick a participant
  - Long-press → delete/leave confirmation
  - Empty state with illustration and CTA
  - No group creation flow; only 1:1 via friend picker

- **`frontend/Rally/src/screens/ChatScreen.tsx:1-260`** — Fully built chat screen:
  - Loads messages with pagination (infinite scroll up)
  - Date separators ("Today", "Yesterday", weekday, full date)
  - Real-time via `useMessaging` hook with REST fallback
  - Typing indicator display, offline banner
  - KeyboardAvoidingView, auto-scroll to bottom
  - Mark-as-read on enter
  - Uses `MessageBubble`, `MessageInput`, `TypingIndicator` sub-components

- **`frontend/Rally/src/services/messagingApi.ts:1-295`** — 11 API methods on `MessagingApiService`:
  - `createThread(participants, title?)`, `getUserThreads()`, `getThreadDetails(threadId)`
  - `getThreadMessages(threadId, limit, offset)`, `sendMessage(threadId, content, messageType)`
  - `markAsRead(threadId)`, `getUnreadCount()`, `getThreadUnreadCount(threadId)`
  - `deleteMessage(messageId)`, `leaveThread(threadId)`, `addParticipants(threadId, participants)`
  - `searchMessages(threadId, query, limit)`
  - All use Bearer token auth from AsyncStorage

- **`frontend/Rally/src/services/socketService.ts:1-340`** — Does NOT handle any chat events. The `SocketEvents` interface covers session, discovery, status, pairing, and game events only. No `messaging:*` events are listed, emitted, or listened to. The chat real-time logic lives in a separate `useMessaging` hook.

- **`frontend/Rally/src/hooks/useMessaging.ts:1-238`** — Separate Socket.io instance for chat:
  - Creates its OWN `io()` connection (not using `socketService` singleton)
  - Listens to: `messaging:new-message`, `messaging:message-deleted`, `messaging:user-typing`, `messaging:user-stopped-typing`, `messaging:messages-read`, `messaging:error`
  - Emits: `messaging:send-message`, `messaging:typing-start`, `messaging:typing-stop`, `messaging:delete-message`, `messaging:mark-read`, `messaging:join-thread`, `messaging:leave-thread`
  - Auto-stop typing after 3s, `auth:identify` on connect
  - **BUG**: Uses `API_BASE_URL` without importing it (line 6: `const SOCKET_URL = API_BASE_URL;`)

- **`frontend/Rally/src/components/ConversationCard.tsx:1-176`** — Rich card: avatar initials, online dot, relative timestamps, message preview, unread badge, chevron. Full UI.

- **`frontend/Rally/src/components/MessageBubble.tsx:1-150`** — Own/other alignment, blue/gray bubbles, sender name on received, read receipts (✓/✓✓), system message style, timestamps. Supports TEXT, IMAGE, SYSTEM, CHALLENGE types but only TEXT and SYSTEM have distinct rendering.

- **`frontend/Rally/src/components/MessageInput.tsx:1-126`** — Text input with send button, multiline, typing indicator debounce (2s), max 5000 chars, keyboard-aware. No attachment/media button.

- **`frontend/Rally/src/components/TypingIndicator.tsx:1-100`** — Animated bouncing dots with "Alice is typing…" / "Alice, Bob are typing…" / "Alice, Bob and 3 others are typing…" text. Fully polished.

---

### CHANGES
None.

### RISKS
- **Dual socket connections**: `useMessaging.ts` creates its own `io()` instance separate from `socketService.ts`. This means two WebSocket connections per client when both chat and session features are active — doubling overhead. Mitigation: consolidate into the singleton `socketService`.
- **`API_BASE_URL` not imported in `useMessaging.ts`**: Line 6 references an undefined variable, which will cause a runtime crash. Mitigation: add `import { API_BASE_URL } from '../config/api';`.
- **No media support**: `MessageType` enum includes `IMAGE` but the `MessageInput` has no attachment button and `MessageBubble` has no image rendering path. Sending images would fail silently or display as blank.
- **Physical delete**: `deleteMessage` does a hard `DELETE FROM messages` rather than soft-delete, so message history can be permanently lost.
- **No group concept**: Threads are identified solely by participant arrays — there's no named group, no group avatar, no admin/owner role. If a 5-person thread needs to become 6, it's possible via `addParticipants`, but the UX doesn't surface this in the chat screen.

### BLOCKERS
None.
